description:
Replace codegen's parallel return-Descr fixpoint with a direct read
of the typer's `module_types.effective_returns`. Single source of
truth across the cont side (already on effective_returns) and the
producer side (today on the buggy clone).

## Root cause (from fz-i82 diagnosis)

`ir_codegen.rs:2403-2494` recomputes per-spec return Descrs and:

  1. Joins `Term::Return` blocks WITHOUT filtering by
     `ft.reachable_blocks` â€” unreachable arms widen the result
     (e.g. `f(:err)` joins block_for_:ok_arm's `any` with `0` and
     publishes `any | 0` â†’ Tagged abi).
  2. Propagates only `Term::TailCall` / `Term::TailCallClosure`,
     NOT `Term::Call` / `Term::CallClosure` / `Term::Receive` with
     a continuation.

`ir_typer::compute_effective_returns` (`module_types.effective_returns`)
already does both correctly. The cont side already reads it
(`cont_slot0_descr:2265`). Only the producer side disagrees.

Effect: the `:err` arm's narrow `0` return gets tag-boxed (`ishl 3;
bor 1` â†’ raw 1) and sent to a cont whose abi expects RawInt 0. The
cont reads `v0 = 1` and prints "1".

## Change

1. `src/ir_codegen.rs`:

   - DELETE `ir_codegen.rs:2403-2494` (the duplicate fixpoint and
     its 11-line fz-ul4.27.13 explanatory block).

   - REPLACE with:
     ```rust
     let return_descrs: Vec<crate::types::Descr> = spec_keys
         .iter()
         .enumerate()
         .map(|(sid, (fid, key))| {
             if spec_fnidx[sid].is_none() {
                 return crate::types::Descr::any(); // sentinel
             }
             let d = module_types
                 .effective_returns
                 .get(&(*fid, key.clone()))
                 .cloned()
                 .unwrap_or_else(crate::types::Descr::any);
             // halt-only specs converge to `none`; `none.is_subtype(float)`
             // makes from_descr pick RawF64. Coerce to `any` so the abi
             // stays Tagged (the value never reaches anyone anyway).
             if d.is_subtype(&crate::types::Descr::none()) {
                 crate::types::Descr::any()
             } else {
                 d
             }
         })
         .collect();
     ```

   - UPDATE `build_typer_header:449-461` similarly: the `@spec` line's
     return-Descr join is replaced with `effective_returns` lookup.
     Threads `&module_types` + `spec_key` into the helper. Keeps
     `@spec` and `@abi` consistent in golden CLIFs.

2. `src/ir_typer.rs:2287-2288` â€” drop the stale "Mirrors the
   codegen-side return-descr fixpoint at `src/ir_codegen.rs:2070-2107`"
   reference; the mirrored code is being deleted.

3. `fixtures/case_tuple_pattern_sequential/`:

   - Frontmatter `paths: [interp, jit, aot]`.
   - Add `expected.clif` + `expected.specs` (BLESSed).
   - Remove the "jit/aot deferred" note from README.

4. Rebless other affected goldens via
   `BLESS=1 cargo test fixture_matrix`. Expected drift:

   - Narrow-return specs that the join had widened: `@abi` flips
     from `Tagged` to `RawInt` (or similar); Return-site CLIF drops
     `ishl_imm 3; bor_imm 1` boxing.
   - Recursive fns' `@spec` widens (`fact(int) -> 1` â†’
     `fact(int) -> int`) because Call+cont propagation now surfaces
     the actual joined return. abi unchanged (both `RawInt`).
   - A handful of `;; v :: <descr>` inline annotations widen.

   Net change in emitted instructions: zero added; some removed.

## What this removes

- ~90 LOC duplicate fixpoint (`ir_codegen.rs:2403-2494`).
- The fz-ul4.27.13 explanatory comment block above it.
- The `ir_typer.rs:2287-2288` stale-link comment.
- The independent return-Descr join in `build_typer_header:449-461`
  (replaced with one lookup).

## Acceptance

- `fixtures/case_tuple_pattern_sequential/` passes on `interp`, `jit`,
  and `aot` paths. Output is `7\\n0\\n0\\n7\\n7\\n0`.
- Reblessed goldens explain themselves (narrower abi / wider @spec
  per the patterns above; no surprise diffs).
- Full `cargo test --release` is green.
- `cargo clippy` clean (warnings are errors).

## Out of scope

- Pruning unreachable per-spec blocks from CLIF emission (further
  tightening â€” own ticket if/when needed).
- Any change to interp; interp doesn't have a return repr.

## Verification commands

```
cargo test --release fixture_matrix
BLESS=1 cargo test --release fixture_matrix     # if goldens drift
cargo test --release
cargo clippy --release --all-targets -- -D warnings
```
title: fz-i82.2: codegen reads module_types.effective_returns; delete duplicate return-Descr fixpoint
